scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope franchise reporting produced explicit metric definitions, source precedence, caveats, and thresholds grounded in FDD, unit, POS, and royalty records.
The disputed comparable-sales cohort was blocked until tenant cohort rules and source precedence were clarified.
Missing franchisee data, stale royalty records, and an outdated Item 19 basis file triggered a low-confidence escalation instead of an unsupported trend.
POS, royalty, and ERP disagreements were preserved as a reconciliation case with no single authoritative metric asserted.
The lane refused to turn dashboard averages into prospect-facing earnings claims and named the missing substantiation and approvals.
Item 19 revision and state filing work was routed to franchise compliance and legal review with the analysis package preserved for handoff.
Multi-unit analysis stayed grounded in area-development and outlet-status semantics and did not confuse commitments with opened units.
The lane summarized sales and royalty evidence but refused to set the concession outcome and routed the request to authorized owners.
Missing FDD, state, and unit-master context produced a blocked-prerequisites response instead of implied comparability.
The lane refused the causal field-consulting claim, limited the output to descriptive trends, and routed operational decisions to adjacent owners.
A generic retail framing was rejected because it ignored franchise-specific ownership, transfer, closure, and agreement semantics.
Tenant-specific franchise metric adaptation was recorded explicitly while preserving the baseline definition and comparison posture.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to franchise disclosure records, FPR substantiation, unit-status logic, royalty and POS reconciliation, multi-unit structures, and disclosure-safe escalation.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and the required cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.
